narrative: Jindal Stainless was the standout ESG performer in FY2024, with 53 rank-one outcomes out of 98 and only 17 rank-four outcomes. Its environmental leadership was broad and structural. It ranked first on Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3, physical GHG intensity and Scope 1+2 turnover intensity, reporting 2.15 tCO2e/tcs and making its Scope 3 disclosure more comparable than in FY2023. It also led on absolute energy, physical energy intensity, water withdrawal, water consumption, zero discharge, air pollutants and absolute waste generation. The BRSR confirms a standalone boundary, reasonable assurance by SGS India and a stainless-focused business with exports of about 17.07% of turnover, reinforcing that the company’s profile is both structurally different and increasingly disclosure-mature. The year was not flawless. Jindal’s training coverage broke sharply across employee and worker H&S and skills, suggesting either reporting-boundary/classification issues or programme-capture weakness. It also lagged on female employee share, wage share, MSME sourcing and some trading-house/procurement indicators. Governance improved significantly through leading sustainability capex at 23.42%, but related-party loans and procurement-route complexity remained watchpoints. Overall, FY2024 shows Jindal as the environmental benchmark, especially for Tata’s decarbonisation and resource-efficiency comparison, while highlighting that Board diversity and environmental intensity do not yet translate into workforce inclusion or training consistency.
